Verified as real Magento ACL resource identifier. Found in Magento 2 menu.xml and documented in official Magento ACL tutorials. Dashboard resource provides access to the admin dashboard.

Verified as real Magento ACL resource identifier. Found in Magento 2 Sales module acl.xml (GitHub: magento/magento2). Provides access to sales operations including orders, invoices, shipments, and credit memos.

Verified as real Magento ACL resource identifier. Found in Magento 2 Customer module acl.xml (GitHub: magento/magento2). Provides access to customer management including the 'All Customers' and 'Now Online' resources.

Verified as Magento ACL concept. Represents full access to all backend resources. When role resources is set to 'All' in the admin panel, it grants access to all actions and areas represented by Magento_Backend::all.

Verified as real Magento ACL resource identifier. Found in Magento 2 Catalog module acl.xml (GitHub: magento/magento2, version 2.4-develop). Provides access to catalog operations including products, categories, and inventory.

Verified as real Magento ACL resource identifier. Referenced in Magento 2 module acl.xml files as parent resource for configuration settings under Magento_Backend::stores_settings. Provides access to system configuration.

privileged_info_summary: The task specifies creation of a Magento admin role named 'Customer Service Lead' and a user 'cs_lead_jones' (Patricia Jones, patricia.jones@example.com). All required resources are verified Magento ACL identifiers: Magento_Backend::dashboard, Magento_Sales::sales, and Magento_Customer::customer are real resources enabling access to dashboard, sales operations, and customer management respectively. All forbidden resources are verified ACL identifiers: Magento_Backend::all (full access), Magento_Catalog::catalog (catalog operations), and Magento_Config::config (system configuration). The role should restrict access to only sales and customer functions while denying catalog and system configuration access.
